excerpt: Doctrine that the human soul, possessing kamāl awwal by nature, attains kamāl thānī only through adherence to the daʿwa and ascent through its ḥudūd — the structural template for the section's recurring 'doubled state' (defective → perfect) trope.
source_location_hint: central doctrine of the work; treated across the mashāriʿ on the soul and its ranks

excerpt: Treatment of the orders of being as they emanate in tiered hierarchies from the One — the conceptual ground for the section's mapping of arithmetical orders (āḥād, ʿashrāt, miʾāt, ulūf) onto cosmological tiers, and for the special status of the tenth at the boundary between orders.
source_location_hint: yanābīʿ treating the structure of emanation and numerical symbolism
